reasons: Esmeralda is strong into shield-heavy melee fights because Freya relies on durability windows to keep attacking.
Freya loses all-in value when her shield-based survivability becomes something Esmeralda can exploit.

proof.summary: Freya wants to survive long enough to keep attacking in melee, while Esmeralda can punish shield-based durability and fight back in extended range.
worksBestWhen: Esmeralda enters after Freya commits to the fight.
Esmeralda has enough durability to stay inside Freya's melee range.
failureCases: Freya bursts Esmeralda before Esmeralda can cycle skills.
Freya avoids extended trades and waits for team follow-up.

reasons: Valir can push Freya away and slow her, reducing her close-range attack uptime.
Freya becomes much weaker when she cannot stay attached during her power window.

proof.summary: Freya wants close-range uptime, while Valir can deny the approach with knockback, slow, and self-protection timing.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds knockback for Freya's jump or all-in.
failureCases: Freya flanks Valir before he can disengage.
Valir wastes knockback before Freya commits.

reasons: Minsitthar can create a zone that restricts directional blink skills, making Freya's engage less flexible.
His field is useful when Freya wants to dive directly into the backline.

proof.summary: Freya wants to commit through movement and melee pressure, while Minsitthar can restrict dash options inside King's Calling.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar places King's Calling after Freya jumps in.
The team focuses Freya while she is trapped in the zone.
failureCases: Freya waits outside the field before committing.
Minsitthar misses the timing or isolates himself.

reasons: Dyrroth can pressure Freya before her extended fight becomes valuable.
His defense reduction and early burst make Freya's lane trades more dangerous.

proof.summary: Freya wants sustained melee fights, while Dyrroth can reduce durability and force a shorter burst trade before Freya stabilizes.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth fights early before Freya scales into items.
Dyrroth has enhanced trading resources ready.
failureCases: Dyrroth overcommits after Freya's defensive window starts.
Freya gets support or shield advantage before Dyrroth bursts.

reasons: Karrie can punish durable melee fighters with repeated marks and true damage.
Freya struggles if she cannot reach Karrie before Karrie builds enough attack speed and spacing safety.

proof.summary: Freya wants to survive through an all-in, while Karrie can cut through durable targets with repeated true-damage marks.
worksBestWhen: Karrie keeps spacing instead of accepting melee trades.
failureCases: Karrie is behind item timing and cannot stack damage quickly.
